negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: RPC async payload validation does not enforce the invariant-required 5KB input ceiling or 30-block TTL ceiling.
related_but_insufficient_code: crates/rpc/rpc/src/async_tx_validator.rs passes MAX_SHORT_ASYNC_TTL_BLOCKS = 500 into validate_async_payload, not 30.
The same validation code does reject malformed ABI, zero executor, zero TTL, and bad HTTP URLs, but with wider size and TTL bounds than required.

notes: The structural validator exists, but the required numeric limits are not ported.
rationale: The new async_tx_validator module defines MAX_TTL_BLOCKS = 30, MAX_ASYNC_INPUT_SIZE = 5 * 1024, validation errors for each rejected condition, HTTP-specific URL checks, and base ExecutorRequest validation for executor and TTL. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: RPC-level async payload validation must enforce input size <= 5KB, decodable ABI payloads, nonzero executor address, ttl greater than 0 and no more than 30 blocks, and for HTTP or long-running HTTP calls a nonempty URL beginning with http:// or https://.
suggested_falsification_tests: Unit-test validate_async_payload across HTTP and LLM payloads: valid payloads pass, oversized payloads fail, malformed ABI fails, zero executor fails, ttl 0 fails, ttl 31 fails, and HTTP payloads with empty or scheme-less URLs fail with the documented error variants.
summary: RPC async payload bounds are wider than required.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Submit an async HTTP payload of 6KB or TTL 31; the invariant expects InvalidParams, while the current validator accepts those bounds if other fields are valid.
title: Async payload constraints enforced
